title: BNP’s First 100 Days Raise Troubling National Questions
gist: Bangladesh's BNP government completed its first 100 days on May 17 amid uncertainty, fear, declining confidence, and diplomatic drift rather than the promised stability and economic recovery, raising doubts about its capacity and vision to govern inclusively.

insights.claim: Bangladesh's BNP government completed its first 100 days on May 17, 2026, but instead of the promised stability, recovery, and renewal, the country appears to be experiencing uncertainty, fear, declining confidence, and diplomatic drift.
insights.speaker: Abul Hasnat Milton
insights.so_what: Early governance signals are negative, raising the political-risk premium on Bangladesh assets and reform trajectory.
insights.locator: As the Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP) government completed

insights.claim: The BNP came to power after months of political upheaval and an interim period under Chief Adviser Muhammad Yunus that critics say weakened institutional continuity and deepened political confrontation.
insights.speaker: Abul Hasnat Milton
insights.so_what: Institutional damage inherited from the transition constrains the new government's ability to deliver stability.
insights.locator: The BNP came to power after months
